footer.leadParagraph: A small team reading the studies, the labels, and the filings — publishing what we'd tell a friend. No product placement. No paid rankings.
footer.imprint: Commissions on some links help fund our testing and never affect our rankings. We update comparisons quarterly.

01.label: Material composition
01.body: Every brand's own filings, not the marketing copy. Which layers are disclosed, which aren't, and what “non-toxic” actually ends up meaning in a claim.

02.label: Heat & abrasion behaviour
02.body: Six weeks on the lab bench. Infrared temperature maps, cast-iron comparison, and the one test most reviewers skip.

03.label: Who disagrees, and why
03.body: We name the critics, link their work, and say where we still disagree. Investigative, not performative.

homeHubs.linkAbout: Why we built it this way →

editorialBoard.eyebrow: How we report · Who reports it
editorialBoard.title: The editorial board.
editorialBoard.lede: Four chairs at the table. Each one can kill a sentence we can't defend.

research.title: The Research Lead
research.bio: Reads the primary literature. Cites the trial, not the press release. Disagreement with the consensus only shows up when the data earns it.

testing.title: The Household Testing Lead
testing.bio: Cooks on every pan for a month. Weighs every bottle. Measures every claim. Tools and notebooks outnumber the opinions.

medical.title: The Medical Reviewer
medical.bio: Flags YMYL claims. Confirms no fearmongering made it past the edit. The person who quietly removes the scary sentence.

sourcing.title: The Sourcing Lead
sourcing.bio: Reads spec sheets. Triangulates manufacturer claims with third-party test data. Names the brands we reject, and says why.

01.q: Which swap are we testing in April?
01.a: Glass water bottles — borosilicate vs soda-lime vs stainless, weighed, dropped, temperature-shocked. Results publish in the May dispatch.

02.q: What brand are we rejecting this month?
02.a: One marketed as PFAS-free whose own MSDS lists a fluoropolymer coating. We'll name it when the full write-up lands, with the filing.

03.q: A reader asked: is our water filter pick outdated?
03.a: The 2024 NSF updates don't change our top recommendation. The runner-up slot is under review — expect a refresh note by end of month.

04.a: Non-toxic mattresses. We've opened the spec sheets for five brands and started reaching out for third-party test data. Publication target: June.

05.q: How do we handle a manufacturer that asks us to retest?
05.a: We retest on our bench, not theirs. If their data contradicts ours, we publish both, name the disagreement, and let the reader see the work.

06.q: Do affiliate links change our rankings?
06.a: They never have. Commissions fund the testing; they don't buy placement. Anything a brand sends us unsolicited goes into the give-away pile, not the review.

title.after: a site where every claim is sourced, every ranking is earned, and every product we reject is named.

01.body: If it isn't in a peer-reviewed paper, a regulatory filing, or a manufacturer disclosure, it doesn't publish.

02.title: Methodology, open.
02.body: Every comparison explains how we picked, what we verified, and who disagrees with us, and why.

03.title: No paid rankings.
03.body: Commissions pay for the testing. They have never, and will never, buy a position on this page.

comparison.changeMindBody: New independent lab testing that contradicts our current ranking. A reformulation by a top pick that quietly drops a disclosed certification. A peer-reviewed paper that changes the safety picture on one of the materials above. We'll update this page within a week and mark what changed.

pillar.pullQuote: We'd rather answer the question you actually asked in the first paragraph, then earn your trust by showing the work.
pillar.keyTakeawayBody: What the best available evidence says today, what's still genuinely uncertain, and what you can actually do this week — in priority order, with every claim sourced.
pillar.faqTitle: The questions people actually bring us.

cluster.shortAnswerBody: The TL;DR sits here for readers who need the answer now. The rest of the page earns that answer — with sources, context, and the things our team debated internally.

picked.label: What we picked for
picked.detail: Disclosed materials, third-party certification, durability in real cooking, independent contamination testing where available.

evaluated.label: How we evaluated
evaluated.detail: Manufacturer disclosures, regulatory filings, peer-reviewed papers, and hands-on wear-testing. We read the labels and the filings, not the press releases.

disagrees.label: Who disagrees with us
disagrees.detail: We steel-man the opposing view in every comparison, and name the brand we almost picked and the reason we didn't.

changeMind.label: What would change our mind
changeMind.detail: New independent lab testing, reformulation by a ranked brand, or a peer-reviewed finding that contradicts our current reasoning.

affiliate.label: Disclosure
affiliate.body: PlasticFreeLab tests and recommends independently. We sometimes earn a commission when you buy through our links — it never affects our rankings.

authorBio.name: The PlasticFreeLab Team
authorBio.body: A small group of researchers and writers cutting through the noise around non-toxic living. We read the studies, read the labels, test the products — and update our recommendations as the science evolves. We do not accept payment for product placement, we disclose every affiliate relationship, and we name the brands we reject.

sources.title: Sources we cited on this page.

notFound.metaDescription: The page you asked for isn't on our index. A few places that might have what you were after.
notFound.title: We couldn't find that page.
notFound.p1: Sometimes a URL changes when we reorganize a hub. Sometimes a post gets retired because the science moved and we'd rather pull it than leave a stale recommendation on the internet.
notFound.p2: Either way, nothing useful lives at this address. The five hubs below cover everything we publish — one of them almost certainly has what you were looking for.

errorPage.title: Something broke on our side.
errorPage.body: Not the reader's fault. A page failed to render the way it was supposed to. Try again — if it keeps happening, head back to the front page and the rest of the site should still behave.

about.title: About PlasticFreeLab
about.metaDescription: Who we are, what we do, and why we built this.
about.intro: PlasticFreeLab exists because the information about non-toxic living is a mess. We built a calm, cited, investigative resource that reads the studies, tests the products, and tells you the three things that actually matter.

editorialStandards.title: Editorial Standards
editorialStandards.metaDescription: Our public-facing editorial policies — sourcing, corrections, affiliate disclosure, AI tooling.
editorialStandards.intro: Every site promises to be trustworthy. Here is what that actually means on PlasticFreeLab — sourcing rules, corrections policy, affiliate disclosure, and our use of AI tooling.

affiliateDisclosure.title: Affiliate Disclosure
affiliateDisclosure.metaDescription: How affiliate links work on PlasticFreeLab and what that means for you.
affiliateDisclosure.intro: Some of the product links on this site are affiliate links. We earn a small commission when you buy through them, at no additional cost to you, and we never let those commissions influence our rankings.

site.tagline: The non-toxic lab for everyday life.
site.description: We test the swaps, read the studies, and publish calm, cited guides to microplastics, PFAS, and the household changes actually worth making.

microplastics-and-edcs.oneLiner: The science, in plain English. What's actually known, what's still debated, and what to do about it.

pipelinePage.subtitle: An open log of every investigation in flight, queued, or in writeup. Updated weekly. We share this because secrecy is for advertorials.
pipelinePage.methodologyFooter: Testing methodology · v1.2 · Last updated {date} · See

methodologyPage.metaDescription: How PlasticFreeLab scores every product. Public methodology, versioned like software.
methodologyPage.intro: Every product on PlasticFreeLab is scored against the same five-dimension rubric. The rubric is public, versioned like software, and updated when better evidence makes our previous version look incomplete.
methodologyPage.currentVersionBody: v1.2, in force since April 2026. Adds a PFAS panel using SGS Method 5021A, expands the durability scoring window from 12 to 24 months, and revises the value calculation to account for warranty terms.
methodologyPage.philosophyP1: If we can't show how we picked, the picks aren't worth much. Every score on this site is a number that any reader can replicate from the same evidence. We publish the dimensions, the weights, the sub-tests, and the lab partners. We disclose what we did not test, and why.
methodologyPage.philosophyP2: Methodology drift is the most common failure mode in product review writing. Our defense against it is the version number. When we change the rubric, we ship a new version, archive the old one, and re-score the affected posts. Readers see exactly what changed.

changeLog.v12: Added PFAS panel using SGS Method 5021A. Expanded durability window 12→24 months. Revised value formula to weight warranty terms.
changeLog.v11: Initial public release. Five-dimension rubric, five reviewer roles, dispute policy.

methodologyPage.disputeBody: If a brand believes our scoring is wrong, they can submit a dispute with documentation. We re-test on our bench, not theirs. If their data contradicts ours, we publish both. We do not edit live scores under threat of legal action; we update them when better evidence arrives.

methodologyV12.metaDescription: The full v1.2 specification: five weighted dimensions, sub-tests, lab partners, reviewer credentials.
methodologyV12.intro: This is the working specification for every product score published on PlasticFreeLab between April 2026 and the next versioned release. Five weighted dimensions, twenty-three sub-tests, four reviewer roles, and a dispute policy that doesn't move under pressure.
methodologyV12.weightsBody: Every score on this site is a weighted average of the five dimensions below. The weights reflect what we believe matters most for a non-toxic-living recommendation: material safety dominates because it's why our reader is here, and value sits last because price is recoverable while exposure is not.

materialSafety.title: Material safety
materialSafety.oneLiner: What it's made of, what it leaches, what's certified.
materialSafety.body: Material safety is what most readers come for and the dimension we weight highest. We score the disclosed bill of materials, third-party certifications (NSF, GOTS, OEKO-TEX, MADE SAFE, Greenguard), and any contaminant test data the manufacturer publishes. Where the manufacturer is silent, we order our own panels through accredited labs.

performance.title: Performance
performance.oneLiner: Does it actually do its job — well — under realistic load.
performance.body: A non-toxic product that doesn't perform is not a recommendation. We measure category-appropriate function: heat distribution and retention for cookware, contaminant reduction and flow rate for filters, structural recovery and breathability for textiles. Performance scores are bench-measured, not vibe-checked.

durability.title: Durability
durability.oneLiner: How well it survives 24 months of normal use.
durability.body: Coatings chip, elastic dies, gaskets shrink. Durability scoring captures the failure modes that turn a non-toxic product into a sub-toxic one twelve months in. Our panel runs each item through a 24-month accelerated wear cycle plus longitudinal field reports from real households.

useExperience.title: Use experience
useExperience.oneLiner: What it's like to live with for a month.
useExperience.body: Weight, balance, cleanability, learning curve, sound, smell. The qualitative dimension — but scored against fixed rubrics so the result reproduces between reviewers. Three panel members each spend a calendar month with the product before scoring.

value.title: Value
value.oneLiner: Cost per year of expected service life — relative to category.
value.body: We score value as expected cost per year of service over a 10-year horizon, normalized against the category median. A pricey item with a 30-year warranty often outscores a cheap item with a 2-year coating. Warranty terms, refurbishment availability, and resale market all feed in.

pfasPanel.title: PFAS panel (SGS Method 5021A)
pfasPanel.body: Quantitative analysis for 40 PFAS compounds at parts-per-billion sensitivity. New in v1.2.

leadCadmium.title: Lead and cadmium screen
leadCadmium.body: Surface-contact extraction per FDA Compliance Policy Guide 7117.06. Pass / numeric value reported.

phthalate.title: Phthalate panel
phthalate.body: GC-MS analysis for the eight phthalates restricted under CPSIA. Reported in micrograms per gram.

vocChamber.title: VOC chamber
vocChamber.body: 72-hour CDPH 01350 chamber test for total VOCs and the priority list of 35 individual compounds.

supplier.title: Supplier disclosure audit
supplier.body: Tier 1 manufacturer published, with country and certification of each material layer.

heatMap.title: Infrared heat map
heatMap.body: Surface temperature mapped at 30-second intervals across a 12-minute heat curve.

deglaze.title: Deglaze + sear panel
deglaze.body: Standardized protein sear plus acidic deglaze, scored on browning, release, and surface integrity.

filterEfficacy.title: Contaminant reduction
filterEfficacy.body: NSF-spec influent challenge water, effluent measured for the ten priority contaminants.

loadCycle.title: Load-cycle test
loadCycle.body: Full-capacity continuous use over the cartridge's stated lifetime, with end-of-life retest.

coatingWear.title: Accelerated coating wear
coatingWear.body: ASTM-style abrasion cycle equivalent to roughly 24 months of normal home use.

warrantyAudit.title: Warranty audit
warrantyAudit.body: Coverage, exclusions, and median claim resolution time pulled from public small-claims filings where available.

fieldReports.title: Longitudinal field reports
fieldReports.body: Six panel households report monthly for 12 months. Mid-test attrition is itself a signal.

weight.title: Weight + ergonomics
weight.body: Mass, balance, and reach for the relevant ergonomic dimensions. Scored against a category baseline.

cleanability.title: Cleanability
cleanability.body: Dishwasher and hand-wash cycles, scored on residue and finish degradation.

panelScores.title: Panel qualitative scoring
panelScores.body: Three reviewers, fixed rubric, blind to brand where the geometry allows it.

costPerYear.title: Expected cost per year
costPerYear.body: Purchase price plus consumables, amortized over expected service life.

warrantyDelta.title: Warranty value adjustment
warrantyDelta.body: Multi-year warranties that pay out reduce the per-year cost; weak warranties don't.

marketComparable.title: Market comparable score
marketComparable.body: Indexed against the median in-category cost-per-year of all current shortlist entrants.

methodologyV12.labPartnersBody: We use SGS, Eurofins, and Intertek for quantitative chemical panels. For PFAS specifically, SGS's Method 5021A is our default. Lab partners are listed as a transparency commitment; partner status doesn't affect scoring, and labs do not see brand identity until the report is filed. (Full partner list and reciprocity policy: to be wired alongside the launch of the v1.3 spec.)
methodologyV12.reviewersIntro: Four roles, each empowered to kill a sentence the team can't defend.

research.title: Research lead
research.bio: Reads the primary literature. Cites the trial, not the press release. Disagreement with the consensus only shows up when the data earns it.

testing.title: Testing lead
testing.bio: Cooks on every pan for a month. Weighs every bottle. Measures every claim. Tools and notebooks outnumber opinions.

medical.title: Medical reviewer
medical.bio: Flags YMYL claims. Confirms no fearmongering made it past the edit. Quietly removes the scary sentence.

sourcing.title: Sourcing lead
sourcing.bio: Reads spec sheets. Triangulates manufacturer claims with third-party test data. Names the brands we reject, and says why.

methodologyV12.disputeBody: If a brand believes our scoring is wrong, they can submit a dispute with documentation. We re-test on our bench, not theirs. If their data contradicts ours, we publish both. We do not edit live scores under threat of legal action; we update them when better evidence arrives.

changeLog.v12: Added PFAS panel via SGS Method 5021A. Expanded durability window from 12 to 24 months. Revised value formula to weight warranty terms.
changeLog.v11: Initial public release. Five-dimension rubric, five reviewer roles, dispute policy.
